124: 		} else {
125: 			m.state = StateEmpty
126: 		}
127: 	}
128: 	m.updateOptions()
129: }
130: 
131: // SetInDiffTab notes whether the diff tab is currently displayed.
132: func (m *Menu) SetInDiffTab(active bool) {
133: 	m.isInDiffTab = active
134: 	m.updateOptions()
135: }
136: 
137: // SetFocusMode toggles the interactive/focus overlay mode.
138: func (m *Menu) SetFocusMode(focused bool) {
139: 	m.isFocusMode = focused
140: 	m.updateOptions()
141: }
142: 
143: // SetFocusSlot records which panel currently holds keyboard focus.
144: func (m *Menu) SetFocusSlot(slot int) {
145: 	m.focusSlot = slot
146: 	m.updateOptions()
147: }
148: 
149: // SetSidebarSpaceAction controls the label shown for the space key while the
150: // sidebar is focused. Accepted values: "expand", "collapse"; others → "toggle".
151: func (m *Menu) SetSidebarSpaceAction(action string) {
152: 	switch action {
153: 	case "expand", "collapse":
154: 		m.sidebarSpaceAction = action
155: 	default:
156: 		m.sidebarSpaceAction = "toggle"
157: 	}
158: }
159: 
160: // SetSize stores the terminal dimensions used for horizontal layout.
161: func (m *Menu) SetSize(width, height int) {
162: 	m.width = width
163: 	m.height = height
164: }
165: 
166: // updateOptions rebuilds the option slice whenever any relevant field changes.
167: func (m *Menu) updateOptions() {
168: 	if m.isFocusMode {
169: 		m.options = []keys.KeyName{keys.KeyExitFocus}
170: 		m.systemGroupSize = 0
171: 		return
172: 	}
173: 	switch m.state {
174: 	case StateEmpty:
175: 		if m.focusSlot == MenuSlotSidebar {
176: 			m.addSidebarOptions(false)
177: 		} else {
178: 			m.options = emptyMenuOptions
179: 			m.systemGroupSize = emptySystemGroupSize
180: 		}
181: 	case StateDefault:
182: 		if m.instance != nil {
183: 			m.addInstanceOptions()
184: 		} else {
185: 			m.addSidebarOptions(true)
186: 		}
187: 	case StateNewInstance:
188: 		m.options = newInstanceMenuOptions
189: 		m.systemGroupSize = 0
190: 	case StatePrompt:
191: 		m.options = promptMenuOptions
192: 		m.systemGroupSize = 0
193: 	}
194: }
195: 
196: // addSidebarOptions populates the option list for sidebar-focused states.
197: // Pass includeNewPlan=true when a plan exists and the 'n' shortcut is relevant.
198: func (m *Menu) addSidebarOptions(includeNewPlan bool) {
199: 	capacity := 8
200: 	if !includeNewPlan {
201: 		capacity--
202: 	}
203: 	opts := make([]keys.KeyName, 0, capacity)
204: 	if includeNewPlan {
205: 		opts = append(opts, keys.KeyNewPlan)
206: 	}
207: 	actionGroup := []keys.KeyName{
208: 		keys.KeyEnter, keys.KeySpaceExpand, keys.KeyViewPlan, keys.KeyAuditToggle,
209: 	}
210: 	systemGroup := []keys.KeyName{
211: 		keys.KeySearch, keys.KeyHelp, keys.KeyQuit,
212: 	}
213: 	opts = append(opts, actionGroup...)
214: 	opts = append(opts, systemGroup...)
215: 	m.options = opts
216: 	m.systemGroupSize = len(systemGroup)
217: }
218: 
219: // addInstanceOptions populates the option list when an instance is selected.
220: func (m *Menu) addInstanceOptions() {
221: 	mgmt := []keys.KeyName{keys.KeyNewPlan, keys.KeyKill}
222: 	action := []keys.KeyName{keys.KeyEnter, keys.KeySendPrompt, keys.KeySpace}
223: 	if m.instance.PromptDetected && m.instance.Status != session.Paused {
224: 		action = append(action, keys.KeySendYes)
225: 	}
226: 	if m.instance.Status == session.Paused {
227: 		action = append(action, keys.KeyResume)
228: 	}
229: 	sys := []keys.KeyName{keys.KeySearch, keys.KeyTab, keys.KeyHelp, keys.KeyQuit}
230: 
231: 	total := len(mgmt) + len(action) + len(sys)
232: 	opts := make([]keys.KeyName, 0, total)
233: 	opts = append(opts, mgmt...)
234: 	opts = append(opts, action...)
235: 	opts = append(opts, sys...)
236: 	m.options = opts
237: 	m.systemGroupSize = len(sys)
238: }
239: 
240: // tmuxCountRendered returns the rendered tmux count badge and its visual width.
241: func (m *Menu) tmuxCountRendered() (string, int) {
242: 	if m.tmuxSessionCount <= 0 {
243: 		return "", 0
244: 	}
245: 	text := statusBarTmuxCountStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("tmux:%d", m.tmuxSessionCount))
246: 	return text, lipgloss.Width(text)
247: }
248: 
249: // placeLine composes a single-row string using cursor-tracked positioning.
250: // It places centeredContent centred on the available width and rightContent
251: // right-aligned, padding with spaces so the total reaches m.width.
252: func (m *Menu) placeLine(
253: 	centeredStart, centeredVisualWidth int, centeredContent string,
254: 	rightStart, rightVisualWidth int, rightContent string,
255: ) string {
256: 	var sb strings.Builder
257: 	pos := 0
258: 
259: 	emit := func(target, visualWidth int, text string) {
260: 		if text == "" || target < pos {
261: 			return
262: 		}
263: 		if target > pos {
264: 			sb.WriteString(strings.Repeat(" ", target-pos))
265: 		}
266: 		sb.WriteString(text)
267: 		pos = target + visualWidth
268: 	}
269: 
270: 	emit(centeredStart, centeredVisualWidth, centeredContent)
271: 	emit(rightStart, rightVisualWidth, rightContent)
272: 
273: 	if pos < m.width {
274: 		sb.WriteString(strings.Repeat(" ", m.width-pos))
275: 	}
276: 	return sb.String()
277: }
278: 
279: // renderFocusMode produces the interactive-mode status row.
280: func (m *Menu) renderFocusMode() string {
281: 	frame := focusModeFrames[int(time.Now().UnixMilli()/100)%len(focusModeFrames)]
282: 	badge := focusLabelStyle.Render("interactive") + " " + focusDotStyle.Render(frame)
283: 	hint := focusHintKeyStyle.Render("ctrl+space") + " " + focusHintDescStyle.Render("exit")
284: 	content := badge + "  " + hint
285: 
286: 	cw := lipgloss.Width(content)
287: 	cs := (m.width - cw) / 2
288: 	if cs < 0 {
289: 		cs = 0
290: 	}
291: 
292: 	tr, tw := m.tmuxCountRendered()
293: 	rs := m.width - tw - 1
294: 	if rs < cs+cw {
295: 		rs = cs + cw
296: 	}
297: 
298: 	return m.placeLine(cs, cw, content, rs, tw, tr)
299: }
300: 
301: // buildOptionText assembles the raw option text (before wrapping in menuStyle).
302: func (m *Menu) buildOptionText() string {
303: 	n := len(m.options)
304: 	if n == 0 {
305: 		return ""
306: 	}
307: 
308: 	// Determine effective system group size.
309: 	sysSize := m.systemGroupSize
310: 	if sysSize == 0 {
311: 		sysSize = defaultSystemGroupSize
312: 	}
313: 	actionEnd := n - sysSize
314: 
315: 	// Group definitions: {start (inclusive), end (exclusive)}.
316: 	type span struct{ start, end int }
317: 	var groups []span
318: 	if m.state == StateEmpty {
319: 		groups = []span{{0, n}}
320: 	} else {
321: 		groups = []span{
322: 			{0, 2},
323: 			{2, actionEnd},
324: 			{actionEnd, n},
325: 		}
326: 	}
327: 
328: 	// isGroupTail reports whether position i is the last element of its group.
329: 	isGroupTail := func(i int) bool {
330: 		for _, g := range groups {
331: 			if i == g.end-1 && i != n-1 {
332: 				return true
333: 			}
334: 		}
335: 		return false
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	// isActionItem reports whether item i belongs to the action group (middle).
339: 	isActionItem := func(i int) bool {
340: 		switch m.state {
341: 		case StateEmpty:
342: 			return i < actionEnd
343: 		default:
344: 			if len(groups) < 2 {
345: 				return false
346: 			}
347: 			return i >= groups[1].start && i < groups[1].end
348: 		}
349: 	}
350: 
351: 	var sb strings.Builder
352: 	for i, k := range m.options {
353: 		binding := keys.GlobalkeyBindings[k]
354: 		h := binding.Help()
355: 		label, desc := h.Key, h.Desc
356: 		if k == keys.KeySpaceExpand {
357: 			desc = m.sidebarSpaceAction
358: 		}
359: 
360: 		// Build per-item styles, optionally underlined for the pressed key.
361: 		akStyle := actionGroupStyle
362: 		kStyle := keyStyle
363: 		dStyle := descStyle
364: 		if m.keyDown == k {
365: 			akStyle = akStyle.Underline(true)
366: 			kStyle = kStyle.Underline(true)
367: 			dStyle = dStyle.Underline(true)
368: 		}
369: 
370: 		if isActionItem(i) {
371: 			sb.WriteString(akStyle.Render(label + " " + desc))
372: 		} else {
373: 			sb.WriteString(kStyle.Render(label))
374: 			sb.WriteString(descStyle.Render(" "))
375: 			sb.WriteString(dStyle.Render(desc))
376: 		}
377: 
378: 		if i < n-1 {
379: 			if isGroupTail(i) {
380: 				sb.WriteString(sepStyle.Render(verticalSeparator))
381: 			} else {
382: 				sb.WriteString(sepStyle.Render(separator))
383: 			}
384: 		}
385: 	}
386: 	return sb.String()
387: }
388: 
389: // String renders the full bottom bar row as a string.
390: func (m *Menu) String() string {
391: 	if m.isFocusMode {
392: 		return m.renderFocusMode()
393: 	}
394: 
395: 	optText := m.buildOptionText()
396: 	rendered := menuStyle.Render(optText)
397: 	rw := lipgloss.Width(rendered)
398: 
399: 	tr, tw := m.tmuxCountRendered()
400: 
401: 	ms := (m.width - rw) / 2
402: 	if ms < 0 {
403: 		ms = 0
404: 	}
405: 	rs := m.width - tw - 1
406: 	if rs < ms+rw {
407: 		rs = ms + rw
408: 	}
409: 
410: 	return m.placeLine(ms, rw, rendered, rs, tw, tr)
411: }

(End of file - total 411 lines)

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package ui
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"path/filepath"
6: 	"strings"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/viewport"
9: 	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
10: 	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/session"
11: 	"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"
12: )
13: 
14: // Exported diff line styles — callers may render individual lines with these.
15: var (
16: 	AdditionStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorDiffAdd)
17: 	DeletionStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorDiffDelete)
18: 	HunkStyle     = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorDiffHunk)
19: )
20: 
21: // Unexported styles used internally by DiffPane rendering.
22: var (
23: 	fileItemStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorIris)
24: 
25: 	fileItemSelectedStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
26: 				Background(ColorIris).
27: 				Foreground(ColorBase).
28: 				Bold(true)
29: 
30: 	fileItemDimStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted)
31: 
32: 	filePanelBorderStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
33: 				Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
34: 				BorderForeground(ColorOverlay)
35: 
36: 	filePanelBorderFocusedStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
37: 					Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
38: 					BorderForeground(ColorIris)
39: 
40: 	diffHeaderStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
41: 			Foreground(ColorIris).
42: 			Bold(true)
43: 
44: 	diffHintStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted)
45: )
46: 
47: // fileChunk holds the parsed diff data for a single file.
48: type fileChunk struct {
49: 	path    string
50: 	added   int
51: 	removed int
52: 	diff    string
53: }
54: 
55: // DiffPane renders a two-column diff view: a file sidebar on the left and a
56: // scrollable diff viewport on the right.
57: type DiffPane struct {
58: 	viewport viewport.Model
59: 	width    int
60: 	height   int
61: 
62: 	files        []fileChunk
63: 	totalAdded   int
64: 	totalRemoved int
65: 	fullDiff     string
66: 
67: 	// selectedFile is -1 for "all files", or a 0-based index into files.
68: 	selectedFile int
69: 
70: 	// sidebarWidth is the total outer width of the sidebar (content + border).
71: 	sidebarWidth int
72: }
73: 
74: // NewDiffPane creates a DiffPane ready for use.
75: func NewDiffPane() *DiffPane {
76: 	return &DiffPane{
77: 		viewport:     viewport.New(0, 0),
78: 		selectedFile: 0,
79: 	}
80: }
81: 
82: // SetSize updates the pane dimensions and re-flows the layout.
83: func (d *DiffPane) SetSize(width, height int) {
84: 	d.width = width
85: 	d.height = height
86: 	d.computeSidebarWidth()
87: 	d.updateViewportWidth()
88: 	d.viewport.Height = height
89: 	d.rebuildViewport()
90: }
91: 
92: // computeSidebarWidth calculates the sidebar outer width from the longest file
93: // entry, capped at 35 % of the total pane width.
94: func (d *DiffPane) computeSidebarWidth() {
95: 	hframe := filePanelBorderStyle.GetHorizontalFrameSize()
96: 
97: 	innerMin := 18
98: 	innerMax := innerMin
99: 	for _, f := range d.files {
100: 		base := filepath.Base(f.path)
101: 		statsLen := len(fmt.Sprintf(" +%d -%d", f.added, f.removed))
102: 		needed := runewidth.StringWidth(base) + statsLen + 4
103: 		if needed > innerMax {
104: 			innerMax = needed
105: 		}
106: 	}
107: 
108: 	cap := d.width*35/100 - hframe
109: 	if cap < innerMin {
110: 		cap = innerMin
111: 	}
112: 	if innerMax > cap {
113: 		innerMax = cap
114: 	}
115: 	d.sidebarWidth = innerMax + hframe
116: }
117: 
118: // updateViewportWidth sets the diff viewport width from the remaining space.
119: func (d *DiffPane) updateViewportWidth() {
120: 	w := d.width - d.sidebarWidth - 1
121: 	if w < 10 {
122: 		w = 10
123: 	}
124: 	d.viewport.Width = w
125: }
126: 
127: // SetDiff loads diff data from the given instance into the pane.
128: func (d *DiffPane) SetDiff(instance *session.Instance) {
129: 	if instance == nil || !instance.Started() {
130: 		d.files = nil
131: 		d.fullDiff = ""
132: 		return
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	stats := instance.GetDiffStats()
136: 	if stats == nil || stats.Error != nil || stats.IsEmpty() {
137: 		d.files = nil
138: 		d.fullDiff = ""
139: 		if stats != nil && stats.Error != nil {
140: 			d.fullDiff = fmt.Sprintf("Error: %v", stats.Error)
141: 		}
142: 		return
143: 	}
144: 
145: 	d.totalAdded = stats.Added
146: 	d.totalRemoved = stats.Removed
147: 	d.files = parseFileChunks(stats.Content)
148: 	d.fullDiff = colorizeDiff(stats.Content)
149: 
150: 	// Clamp selectedFile into the valid range.
151: 	last := len(d.files) - 1
152: 	if d.selectedFile > last {
153: 		d.selectedFile = last
154: 	}
155: 	if d.selectedFile < -1 {
156: 		d.selectedFile = -1
157: 	}
158: 
159: 	d.computeSidebarWidth()
160: 	d.updateViewportWidth()
161: 	d.rebuildViewport()
162: }
163: 
164: // rebuildViewport reloads the viewport content for the active selection.
165: func (d *DiffPane) rebuildViewport() {
166: 	if len(d.files) == 0 {
167: 		return
168: 	}
169: 	var content string
170: 	switch {
171: 	case d.selectedFile < 0:
172: 		content = d.fullDiff
173: 	case d.selectedFile < len(d.files):
174: 		content = colorizeDiff(d.files[d.selectedFile].diff)
175: 	}
176: 	d.viewport.SetContent(content)
177: }
178: 
179: // String returns the rendered pane as a string.
180: func (d *DiffPane) String() string {
181: 	if len(d.files) == 0 {
182: 		msg := "No changes"
183: 		if d.fullDiff != "" {
184: 			msg = d.fullDiff
185: 		}
186: 		return lipgloss.Place(d.width, d.height, lipgloss.Center, lipgloss.Center, msg)
187: 	}
188: 	sidebar := d.renderSidebar()
189: 	right := d.viewport.View()
190: 	return lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, sidebar, " ", right)
191: }
192: 
193: // renderSidebar builds the bordered file-list panel.
194: func (d *DiffPane) renderSidebar() string {
195: 	hframe := filePanelBorderStyle.GetHorizontalFrameSize()
196: 	innerW := d.sidebarWidth - hframe
197: 
198: 	var sb strings.Builder
199: 
200: 	// Header — coloured totals.
201: 	addStr := AdditionStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("+%d", d.totalAdded))
202: 	delStr := DeletionStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("-%d", d.totalRemoved))
203: 	sb.WriteString(addStr + " " + delStr + "\n")
204: 
205: 	// "All" entry.
206: 	allLabel := "\uf0ce All"
207: 	if d.selectedFile == -1 {
208: 		sb.WriteString(fileItemSelectedStyle.Width(innerW).Render(" " + allLabel))
209: 	} else {
210: 		sb.WriteString(fileItemStyle.Render(" " + allLabel))
211: 	}
212: 	sb.WriteString("\n")
213: 
214: 	// Per-file entries.
215: 	for idx, f := range d.files {
216: 		base := filepath.Base(f.path)
217: 		dir := filepath.Dir(f.path)
218: 		selected := idx == d.selectedFile
219: 
220: 		// Build plain stats string for width calculations.
221: 		var plainStats string
222: 		if f.added > 0 {
223: 			plainStats += fmt.Sprintf("+%d", f.added)
224: 		}
225: 		if f.removed > 0 {
226: 			if plainStats != "" {
227: 				plainStats += " "
228: 			}
229: 			plainStats += fmt.Sprintf("-%d", f.removed)
230: 		}
231: 
232: 		if selected {
233: 			maxName := innerW - runewidth.StringWidth(plainStats) - 3
234: 			name := base
235: 			if maxName > 3 && runewidth.StringWidth(name) > maxName {
236: 				name = runewidth.Truncate(name, maxName, "…")
237: 			}
238: 			line := fmt.Sprintf(" %s %s", name, plainStats)
239: 			sb.WriteString(fileItemSelectedStyle.Width(innerW).Render(line))
240: 		} else {
241: 			maxName := innerW - runewidth.StringWidth(plainStats) - 3
242: 
243: 			// Build coloured name with optional dim dir prefix.
244: 			var nameDisplay string
245: 			if dir == "." {
246: 				name := base
247: 				if maxName > 3 && runewidth.StringWidth(name) > maxName {
248: 					name = runewidth.Truncate(name, maxName, "…")
249: 				}
250: 				nameDisplay = fileItemStyle.Render(name)
251: 			} else {
252: 				dirPrefix := dir + "/"
253: 				remaining := maxName - runewidth.StringWidth(dirPrefix)
254: 				if remaining < 4 {
255: 					name := base
256: 					if maxName > 3 && runewidth.StringWidth(name) > maxName {
257: 						name = runewidth.Truncate(name, maxName, "…")
258: 					}
259: 					nameDisplay = fileItemStyle.Render(name)
260: 				} else {
261: 					name := base
262: 					if runewidth.StringWidth(name) > remaining {
263: 						name = runewidth.Truncate(name, remaining, "…")
264: 					}
265: 					nameDisplay = fileItemDimStyle.Render(dirPrefix) + fileItemStyle.Render(name)
266: 				}
267: 			}
268: 
269: 			// Build coloured stats.
270: 			var coloredStats string
271: 			if f.added > 0 {
272: 				coloredStats += AdditionStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("+%d", f.added))
273: 			}
274: 			if f.removed > 0 {
275: 				if coloredStats != "" {
276: 					coloredStats += " "
277: 				}
278: 				coloredStats += DeletionStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("-%d", f.removed))
279: 			}
280: 
281: 			sb.WriteString(" " + nameDisplay + " " + coloredStats)
282: 		}
283: 		sb.WriteString("\n")
284: 	}
285: 
286: 	// Pad to fill available height (border + hint accounted for).
287: 	occupied := 2 + len(d.files) // header line + "All" + file entries
288: 	for row := occupied; row < d.height-3; row++ {
289: 		sb.WriteString("\n")
290: 	}
291: 
292: 	// Navigation hint at the bottom.
293: 	sb.WriteString(diffHintStyle.Render("shift+↑↓"))
294: 
295: 	vframe := filePanelBorderStyle.GetVerticalFrameSize()
296: 	innerH := d.height - vframe
297: 	if innerH < 1 {
298: 		innerH = 1
299: 	}
300: 	return filePanelBorderStyle.Width(innerW).Height(innerH).Render(sb.String())
301: }
302: 
303: // FileUp moves the sidebar selection one entry upward (with wrap-around).
304: func (d *DiffPane) FileUp() {
305: 	if len(d.files) == 0 {
306: 		return
307: 	}
308: 	d.selectedFile--
309: 	if d.selectedFile < -1 {
310: 		d.selectedFile = len(d.files) - 1
311: 	}
312: 	d.rebuildViewport()
313: 	d.viewport.GotoTop()
314: }
315: 
316: // FileDown moves the sidebar selection one entry downward (with wrap-around).
317: func (d *DiffPane) FileDown() {
318: 	if len(d.files) == 0 {
319: 		return
320: 	}
321: 	d.selectedFile++
322: 	if d.selectedFile >= len(d.files) {
323: 		d.selectedFile = -1
324: 	}
325: 	d.rebuildViewport()
326: 	d.viewport.GotoTop()
327: }
328: 
329: // ScrollUp scrolls the diff viewport up by 3 lines.
330: func (d *DiffPane) ScrollUp() {
331: 	d.viewport.LineUp(3)
332: }
333: 
334: // ScrollDown scrolls the diff viewport down by 3 lines.
335: func (d *DiffPane) ScrollDown() {
336: 	d.viewport.LineDown(3)
337: }

339: // HasFiles reports whether any diff files are loaded.
340: func (d *DiffPane) HasFiles() bool {
341: 	return len(d.files) > 0
342: }
343: 
344: // parseFileChunks splits a unified diff into per-file chunks, counting
345: // added/removed lines in each chunk.
346: func parseFileChunks(content string) []fileChunk {
347: 	var chunks []fileChunk
348: 	var cur *fileChunk
349: 	var buf strings.Builder
350: 
351: 	for _, line := range strings.Split(content, "\n") {
352: 		if strings.HasPrefix(line, "diff --git ") {
353: 			// Flush previous chunk.
354: 			if cur != nil {
355: 				cur.diff = buf.String()
356: 				buf.Reset()
357: 			}
358: 			// Extract the b/ path from "diff --git a/<path> b/<path>".
359: 			path := ""
360: 			if parts := strings.SplitN(line, " b/", 2); len(parts) == 2 {
361: 				path = parts[1]
362: 			}
363: 			chunks = append(chunks, fileChunk{path: path})
364: 			cur = &chunks[len(chunks)-1]
365: 			buf.WriteString(line + "\n")
366: 			continue
367: 		}
368: 		if cur == nil {
369: 			continue
370: 		}
371: 		buf.WriteString(line + "\n")
372: 		switch {
373: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "+") && !strings.HasPrefix(line, "+++"):
374: 			cur.added++
375: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "-") && !strings.HasPrefix(line, "---"):
376: 			cur.removed++
377: 		}
378: 	}
379: 	if cur != nil {
380: 		cur.diff = buf.String()
381: 	}
382: 	return chunks
383: }
384: 
385: // colorizeDiff applies ANSI colour styles to each line of a unified diff.
386: func colorizeDiff(diff string) string {
387: 	lines := strings.Split(diff, "\n")
388: 	var out strings.Builder
389: 	out.Grow(len(diff) + len(lines)*8)
390: 	for _, line := range lines {
391: 		if len(line) == 0 {
392: 			out.WriteByte('\n')
393: 			continue
394: 		}
395: 		switch {
396: 		case strings.HasPrefix(line, "@@"):
397: 			out.WriteString(HunkStyle.Render(line))
398: 		case line[0] == '+' && (len(line) == 1 || line[1] != '+'):
399: 			out.WriteString(AdditionStyle.Render(line))
400: 		case line[0] == '-' && (len(line) == 1 || line[1] != '-'):
401: 			out.WriteString(DeletionStyle.Render(line))
402: 		default:
403: 			out.WriteString(line)
404: 		}
405: 		out.WriteByte('\n')
406: 	}
407: 	return out.String()
408: }

13: // AuditEventDisplay is a pre-formatted event record for the audit pane.

21: }
22:

27: 	width    int

76: 
77: // Visible reports whether the pane is shown.
78: func (p *AuditPane) Visible() bool { return p.visible }
79: 
80: // Height returns the total height (header + body) set by SetSize.
81: func (p *AuditPane) Height() int { return p.height }
82: 
83: // ToggleVisible flips the visibility flag.
84: func (p *AuditPane) ToggleVisible() { p.visible = !p.visible }
85: 
86: // Audit-pane styles — Rosé Pine Moon palette.
87: var (
88: 	auditDividerStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted)
89: 	auditMinuteStyle  = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted)
90: 	auditMsgStyle     = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorSubtle)
91: 	auditWarnMsgStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorGold)
92: 	auditErrMsgStyle  = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorLove)
93: 	auditEmptyStyle   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted)
94: 	auditRowPad       = lipgloss.NewStyle().PaddingLeft(1)
95: )
96: 
97: // String returns the full rendered output: header divider + viewport body.
98: func (p *AuditPane) String() string {
99: 	return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, p.renderHeader(), p.viewport.View())
100: }
101: 
102: // renderHeader builds the centred "────── log ──────" divider line.
103: func (p *AuditPane) renderHeader() string {
104: 	const label = " log "
105: 	remaining := p.width - len(label)
106: 	if remaining < 2 {
107: 		return auditDividerStyle.Render(label)
108: 	}
109: 	left := remaining / 2
110: 	right := remaining - left
111: 	return auditDividerStyle.Render(
112: 		strings.Repeat("─", left) + label + strings.Repeat("─", right),
113: 	)
114: }
115: 
116: // renderMinuteHeader builds a centred "────── HH:MM ──────" divider.
117: func (p *AuditPane) renderMinuteHeader(minute string) string {
118: 	inner := " " + minute + " "
119: 	remaining := p.width - len(inner)
120: 	if remaining < 2 {
121: 		return auditMinuteStyle.Render(inner)
122: 	}
123: 	left := remaining / 2
124: 	right := remaining - left
125: 	return auditMinuteStyle.Render(
126: 		strings.Repeat("─", left) + inner + strings.Repeat("─", right),
127: 	)
128: }
129: 
130: // renderBody builds the scrollable event list.
131: //
132: // Layout per event row:
133: //
134: //	" ◆  message text"
135: //	 1 1 2 = 4 chars of overhead before message
136: //
137: // Continuation lines from word-wrapped messages are indented by the same
138: // overhead so they align under the first character of the message text.
139: func (p *AuditPane) renderBody() string {
140: 	if len(p.events) == 0 {
141: 		return auditRowPad.Render(auditEmptyStyle.Render("· no events"))
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	const overhead = 4
145: 	msgW := p.width - overhead
146: 	if msgW < 10 {
147: 		msgW = 10
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	// Walk events newest-first so that we can detect minute boundaries, then
151: 	// append in reverse order so oldest events end up at the top of the output.
152: 	lines := make([]string, 0, len(p.events))
153: 	var lastMinute string
154: 
155: 	for i := len(p.events) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
156: 		e := p.events[i]
157: 
158: 		// Emit a centred minute header whenever the minute value changes.
159: 		if e.Time != lastMinute {
160: 			lines = append(lines, p.renderMinuteHeader(e.Time))
161: 			lastMinute = e.Time
162: 		}
163: 
164: 		icon := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(e.Color).Render(e.Icon)
165: 		wrapped := wordwrap.String(e.Message, msgW)
166: 		segments := strings.Split(wrapped, "\n")
167: 
168: 		for j, seg := range segments {
169: 			var styledSeg string
170: 			switch e.Level {
171: 			case "error":
172: 				styledSeg = auditErrMsgStyle.Render(seg)
173: 			case "warn":
174: 				styledSeg = auditWarnMsgStyle.Render(seg)
175: 			default:
176: 				styledSeg = auditMsgStyle.Render(seg)
177: 			}
178: 
179: 			var row string
180: 			if j == 0 {
181: 				row = auditRowPad.Render(icon + "  " + styledSeg)
182: 			} else {
183: 				row = auditRowPad.Render(strings.Repeat(" ", overhead) + styledSeg)
184: 			}
185: 			lines = append(lines, row)
186: 		}
187: 	}
188: 
189: 	return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
190: }
191: 
192: // EventKindIcon maps an event kind string to a display glyph and colour.
193: // Used by the app layer when constructing AuditEventDisplay values.
194: func EventKindIcon(kind string) (icon string, color lipgloss.Color) {
195: 	switch kind {
196: 	case "agent_spawned":
197: 		return "◆", ColorFoam
198: 	case "agent_finished":
199: 		return "✓", ColorGold
200: 	case "agent_killed":
201: 		return "✕", ColorLove
202: 	case "agent_paused":
203: 		return "⏸", ColorMuted
204: 	case "agent_resumed":
205: 		return "▶", ColorFoam
206: 	case "plan_transition":
207: 		return "⟳", ColorIris
208: 	case "plan_created":
209: 		return "✦", ColorFoam
210: 	case "plan_merged":
211: 		return "⇒", ColorGold
212: 	case "plan_cancelled":
213: 		return "✕", ColorLove
214: 	case "wave_started":
215: 		return "⚡", ColorGold
216: 	case "wave_completed":
217: 		return "⚡", ColorFoam
218: 	case "wave_failed":
219: 		return "⚡", ColorLove
220: 	case "prompt_sent":
221: 		return "→", ColorFoam
222: 	case "git_push":
223: 		return "↑", ColorFoam
224: 	case "pr_created":
225: 		return "⎇", ColorIris
226: 	case "permission_detected":
227: 		return "!", ColorGold
228: 	case "permission_answered":
229: 		return "✓", ColorGold
230: 	case "session_started":
231: 		return "▶", ColorFoam
232: 	case "session_stopped":
233: 		return "■", ColorMuted
234: 	case "fsm_error", "error":
235: 		return "!", ColorLove
236: 	default:
237: 		return "·", ColorMuted
238: 	}
239: }
240: 
241: // debugAudit appends a formatted line to /tmp/audit_debug.log.
242: // Fire-and-forget; errors are intentionally ignored.
243: func debugAudit(format string, args ...any) {
244: 	f, err := os.OpenFile("/tmp/audit_debug.log", os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
245: 	if err != nil {
246: 		return
247: 	}
248: 	defer f.Close()
249: 	fmt.Fprintf(f, format+"\n", args...)
250: }

(End of file - total 250 lines)

package ui

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"strings"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/viewport"

	"github.com/muesli/reflow/wordwrap"
)

// AuditEventDisplay is a pre-formatted event record for the audit pane.
type AuditEventDisplay struct {
	Time    string         // wall-clock time formatted as "HH:MM"
	Kind    string         // event kind string (e.g. "agent_spawned")
	Icon    string         // single-character icon glyph
	Message string         // human-readable event description
	Color   lipgloss.Color // icon foreground colour
	Level   string         // "info", "warn", or "error"

20: // previewState holds the current display state of the preview pane.
21: type previewState struct {
22: 	// fallback indicates the pane is in fallback (no active session) mode.
23: 	fallback bool
24: 	// fallbackMsg is shown below the banner in banner-fallback mode.
25: 	fallbackMsg string
26: 	// text holds the raw content to display in normal or fallback-content modes.
27: 	text string
28: }
29: 
30: // PreviewPane renders the agent session preview area.
31: type PreviewPane struct {
32: 	width  int
33: 	height int
34: 
35: 	previewState previewState
36: 	isScrolling  bool
37: 	viewport     viewport.Model
38: 
39: 	// bannerFrame is the current animation tick index for the idle banner.
40: 	bannerFrame int
41: 	// animateBanner enables the idle banner ticker when true.
42: 	animateBanner bool
43: 	// isDocument is true while showing a rendered plan document.
44: 	// UpdateContent is a no-op when this flag is set.
45: 	isDocument bool
46: 	// isRawTerminal is true when content was pushed via SetRawContent.
47: 	// The VT emulator already fills exactly p.height rows, so String() must
48: 	// not subtract 1 for an ellipsis row or the last line gets dropped.
49: 	isRawTerminal bool
50: 	// springAnim drives the banner load-in animation on first render.
51: 	springAnim *SpringAnim
52: }
53: 
54: // NewPreviewPane constructs a PreviewPane with initial fallback state.
55: func NewPreviewPane() *PreviewPane {
56: 	return &PreviewPane{
57: 		viewport:   viewport.New(0, 0),
58: 		springAnim: NewSpringAnim(6.0, 15),
59: 		previewState: previewState{
60: 			fallback:    true,
61: 			fallbackMsg: "create [n]ew plan or select existing",
62: 		},
63: 	}
64: }
65: 
66: // TickSpring advances the spring load-in animation by one frame.
67: func (p *PreviewPane) TickSpring() {
68: 	if p.springAnim != nil {
69: 		p.springAnim.Tick()
70: 	}
71: }
72: 
73: // SetRawContent sets preview content from a pre-rendered string (VT emulator path).
74: // Clears scroll, document, and fallback flags and marks the pane as raw-terminal.
75: func (p *PreviewPane) SetRawContent(content string) {
76: 	p.previewState = previewState{text: content}
77: 	p.isScrolling = false
78: 	p.isDocument = false
79: 	p.isRawTerminal = true
80: }
81: 
82: // SetSize stores the pane dimensions and configures the viewport.
83: // The viewport width is width-1 to reserve one column for the scrollbar.
84: func (p *PreviewPane) SetSize(width, maxHeight int) {
85: 	p.width = width
86: 	p.height = maxHeight
87: 	p.viewport.Width = max(0, width-1)
88: 	p.viewport.Height = maxHeight
89: }

91: // setFallbackState puts the pane into banner+message fallback mode.
92: func (p *PreviewPane) setFallbackState(message string) {
93: 	p.previewState = previewState{
94: 		fallback:    true,
95: 		fallbackMsg: message,
96: 	}
97: 	p.isRawTerminal = false
98: }
99: 
100: // SetDocumentContent loads scrollable document content into the viewport.
101: // The pane remains in document mode (UpdateContent is a no-op) until
102: // ClearDocumentMode is called.
103: func (p *PreviewPane) SetDocumentContent(content string) {
104: 	p.previewState = previewState{fallback: false}
105: 	p.isScrolling = false
106: 	p.isDocument = true
107: 	p.isRawTerminal = false
108: 	p.viewport.SetContent(content)
109: 	p.viewport.GotoTop()

111: 
112: // IsDocumentMode reports whether the pane is displaying a static document.
113: func (p *PreviewPane) IsDocumentMode() bool {
114: 	return p.isDocument
115: }
116: 
117: // ClearDocumentMode exits document mode so UpdateContent resumes normal preview.
118: func (p *PreviewPane) ClearDocumentMode() {

120: }
121: 
122: // ViewportUpdate forwards a tea.Msg to the viewport when in document or scroll
123: // mode, enabling native viewport key handling (PgUp/PgDn, arrows, mouse wheel).
124: // Returns nil when the pane is not in a scrollable mode.
125: func (p *PreviewPane) ViewportUpdate(msg tea.Msg) tea.Cmd {
126: 	if !p.isDocument && !p.isScrolling {
127: 		return nil
128: 	}
129: 	var cmd tea.Cmd
130: 	p.viewport, cmd = p.viewport.Update(msg)
131: 	return cmd
132: }
133: 
134: // ViewportHandlesKey reports whether the viewport keymap matches the given key
135: // when the pane is in document or scroll mode.
136: func (p *PreviewPane) ViewportHandlesKey(msg tea.KeyMsg) bool {
137: 	if !p.isDocument && !p.isScrolling {
138: 		return false
139: 	}
140: 	km := p.viewport.KeyMap
141: 	return key.Matches(msg,
142: 		km.Up, km.Down, km.Left, km.Right,
143: 		km.PageUp, km.PageDown,
144: 		km.HalfPageUp, km.HalfPageDown,
145: 	)
146: }
147: 
148: // setFallbackContent sets fallback mode with arbitrary centered content (no banner).
149: func (p *PreviewPane) setFallbackContent(content string) {
150: 	p.previewState = previewState{
151: 		fallback: true,
152: 		text:     content,
153: 	}
154: 	p.isRawTerminal = false
155: }
156: 
157: // SetAnimateBanner enables or disables the idle banner animation ticker.
158: func (p *PreviewPane) SetAnimateBanner(enabled bool) {
159: 	p.animateBanner = enabled
160: }
161: 
162: // TickBanner advances the banner animation frame. Call from the app tick loop.
163: func (p *PreviewPane) TickBanner() {
164: 	if p.animateBanner && p.previewState.fallback {
165: 		p.bannerFrame++
166: 	}
167: }
168: 
169: // UpdateContent refreshes the pane based on the instance state. It is a no-op
170: // when in document mode. In normal (non-scroll) mode live content arrives via
171: // SetRawContent from the VT emulator; this method only handles nil/Loading/
172: // Paused/Exited special cases plus initial scroll-mode capture.
173: func (p *PreviewPane) UpdateContent(instance *session.Instance) error {
174: 	if p.isDocument {
175: 		return nil
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	switch {
179: 	case instance == nil:
180: 		p.setFallbackState("create [n]ew plan or select existing")
181: 		return nil
182: 
183: 	case instance.Status == session.Loading:
184: 		stage := instance.LoadingStage
185: 		total := instance.LoadingTotal
186: 		if total == 0 {
187: 			total = 7
188: 		}
189: 		barWidth := 20
190: 		filled := (stage * barWidth) / total
191: 		if filled > barWidth {
192: 			filled = barWidth
193: 		}
194: 		bar := GradientBar(barWidth, filled, GradientStart, GradientEnd)
195: 
196: 		stepText := instance.LoadingMessage
197: 		if stepText == "" {
198: 			stepText = "Starting..."
199: 		}
200: 		pct := 0
201: 		if total > 0 {
202: 			pct = (stage * 100) / total
203: 		}
204: 
205: 		p.setFallbackContent(lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Center,
206: 			"",
207: 			lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color(GradientStart)).Render("Starting instance"),
208: 			"",
209: 			bar,
210: 			"",
211: 			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted).Render(stepText),
212: 			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted).Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d%%", pct)),
213: 		))
214: 		return nil
215: 
216: 	case instance.Status == session.Paused:
217: 		p.setFallbackContent(lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Center,
218: 			"Session is paused. Press 'r' to resume.",
219: 			"",
220: 			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorGold).Render(fmt.Sprintf(
221: 				"The instance can be checked out at '%s' (copied to your clipboard)",
222: 				instance.Branch,
223: 			)),
224: 		))
225: 		return nil
226: 
227: 	case instance.Exited:
228: 		p.setFallbackContent(lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Center,
229: 			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted).Render("session exited"),
230: 			"",
231: 			lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted).Render("press shift+k to remove"),
232: 		))
233: 		return nil
234: 	}
235: 
236: 	// If in scroll mode but haven't loaded content yet, capture full history now.
237: 	if p.isScrolling && p.viewport.Height > 0 && len(p.viewport.View()) == 0 {
238: 		content, err := instance.PreviewFullHistory()
239: 		if err != nil {
240: 			return err
241: 		}
242: 		footer := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted).Render("ESC to exit scroll mode")
243: 		p.viewport.SetContent(lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, content, footer))
244: 	}
245: 	// Normal mode: live content arrives via SetRawContent from the VT emulator.
246: 	return nil
247: }
248: 
249: // renderScrollbar builds a vertical scrollbar string for the given height.
250: // Returns an empty string when all content fits on screen (no scrolling needed).
251: func (p *PreviewPane) renderScrollbar(height int) string {
252: 	if height <= 0 {
253: 		return ""
254: 	}
255: 	// Hide scrollbar when everything fits on one screen.
256: 	if p.viewport.AtBottom() && p.viewport.YOffset == 0 {
257: 		return ""
258: 	}
259: 
260: 	pct := p.viewport.ScrollPercent()
261: 	thumbSize := max(1, height/5)
262: 	trackLen := height - thumbSize
263: 	thumbPos := int(pct * float64(trackLen))
264: 
265: 	var sb strings.Builder
266: 	for i := range height {
267: 		if i >= thumbPos && i < thumbPos+thumbSize {
268: 			sb.WriteString(scrollbarThumbStyle.Render("▐"))
269: 		} else {
270: 			sb.WriteString(scrollbarTrackStyle.Render("│"))
271: 		}
272: 		if i < height-1 {
273: 			sb.WriteByte('\n')
274: 		}
275: 	}
276: 	return sb.String()
277: }
278: 
279: // String renders the preview pane to a string.
280: func (p *PreviewPane) String() string {
281: 	if p.width == 0 || p.height == 0 {
282: 		return strings.Repeat("\n", p.height)
283: 	}
284: 
285: 	if p.previewState.fallback {
286: 		fallbackText := p.buildFallbackText()
287: 		return lipgloss.Place(
288: 			p.width, p.height,
289: 			lipgloss.Center, lipgloss.Center,
290: 			previewPaneStyle.Render(fallbackText),
291: 		)
292: 	}
293: 
294: 	// Document or scroll mode: render via viewport + optional scrollbar.
295: 	if p.isDocument || p.isScrolling {
296: 		viewContent := p.viewport.View()
297: 		scrollbar := p.renderScrollbar(p.viewport.Height)
298: 		if scrollbar == "" {
299: 			return viewContent
300: 		}
301: 		return lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, viewContent, scrollbar)
302: 	}
303: 
304: 	// Normal mode: split text, truncate/pad, render.
305: 	availableHeight := p.height
306: 	if !p.isRawTerminal {
307: 		availableHeight-- // reserve one row for the ellipsis overflow indicator
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	lines := strings.Split(p.previewState.text, "\n")
311: 	if availableHeight > 0 {
312: 		if len(lines) > availableHeight {
313: 			lines = lines[:availableHeight]
314: 			if !p.isRawTerminal {
315: 				lines = append(lines, "...")
316: 			}
317: 		} else {
318: 			padding := availableHeight - len(lines)
319: 			lines = append(lines, make([]string, padding)...)
320: 		}
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	return previewPaneStyle.Width(p.width).Render(strings.Join(lines, "\n"))
324: }
325: 
326: // buildFallbackText constructs the text for fallback (no active session) rendering.
327: func (p *PreviewPane) buildFallbackText() string {
328: 	// Content fallback (loading spinner, paused state, exited, etc.)
329: 	if p.previewState.fallbackMsg == "" {
330: 		if p.previewState.text != "" {
331: 			return p.previewState.text
332: 		}
333: 		// Banner only — no CTA message.
334: 		bannerLines := BannerLines(p.bannerFrame)
335: 		if p.springAnim != nil && !p.springAnim.Settled() {
336: 			visibleRows := p.springAnim.VisibleRows()
337: 			if visibleRows <= 0 {
338: 				return ""
339: 			}
340: 			total := len(bannerLines)
341: 			start := (total - visibleRows) / 2
342: 			end := start + visibleRows
343: 			if start < 0 {
344: 				start = 0
345: 			}
346: 			if end > total {
347: 				end = total
348: 			}
349: 			return strings.Join(bannerLines[start:end], "\n")
350: 		}
351: 		return FallBackText(p.bannerFrame)
352: 	}
353: 
354: 	// Banner + CTA message fallback.
355: 	bannerLines := BannerLines(p.bannerFrame)
356: 	animating := p.springAnim != nil && !p.springAnim.Settled()
357: 	visibleRows := len(bannerLines)
358: 	if animating {
359: 		visibleRows = p.springAnim.VisibleRows()
360: 	}
361: 
362: 	if visibleRows <= 0 {
363: 		// Reserve stable space while spring starts up.
364: 		bannerWidth := lipgloss.Width(bannerLines[0])
365: 		blankBanner := strings.Repeat(strings.Repeat(" ", bannerWidth)+"\n", len(bannerLines)-1)
366: 		blankBanner += strings.Repeat(" ", bannerWidth)
367: 		blankCTA := strings.Repeat(" ", lipgloss.Width(p.previewState.fallbackMsg))
368: 		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, blankBanner, "", blankCTA)
369: 	}
370: 
371: 	// Build banner block with hidden rows as blanks to keep height constant.
372: 	totalRows := len(bannerLines)
373: 	startRow := (totalRows - visibleRows) / 2
374: 	endRow := startRow + visibleRows
375: 	if startRow < 0 {
376: 		startRow = 0
377: 	}
378: 	if endRow > totalRows {
379: 		endRow = totalRows
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	bannerWidth := lipgloss.Width(bannerLines[0])
383: 	blankLine := strings.Repeat(" ", bannerWidth)
384: 	bannerParts := make([]string, 0, totalRows)
385: 	for i := range startRow {
386: 		_ = i
387: 		bannerParts = append(bannerParts, blankLine)
388: 	}
389: 	bannerParts = append(bannerParts, bannerLines[startRow:endRow]...)
390: 	for i := range totalRows - endRow {
391: 		_ = i
392: 		bannerParts = append(bannerParts, blankLine)
393: 	}
394: 	banner := strings.Join(bannerParts, "\n")
395: 
396: 	// CTA: horizontal character-by-character reveal after spring delay.
397: 	ctaMsg := p.previewState.fallbackMsg
398: 	ctaRunes := []rune(ctaMsg)
399: 	ctaFullWidth := lipgloss.Width(ctaMsg)
400: 	ctaPad := (bannerWidth - ctaFullWidth) / 2
401: 	if ctaPad < 0 {
402: 		ctaPad = 0
403: 	}
404: 
405: 	if p.springAnim != nil {
406: 		p.springAnim.SetCTALen(len(ctaRunes))
407: 	}
408: 
409: 	visChars := len(ctaRunes) // default: fully visible when settled
410: 	if p.springAnim != nil && !p.springAnim.Settled() {
411: 		visChars = p.springAnim.CTAVisibleChars()
412: 	}
413: 
414: 	switch {
415: 	case visChars <= 0:
416: 		blankCTA := strings.Repeat(" ", ctaFullWidth)
417: 		centeredCTA := strings.Repeat(" ", ctaPad) + blankCTA
418: 		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, banner, "", centeredCTA)
419: 	case visChars >= len(ctaRunes):
420: 		centeredCTA := strings.Repeat(" ", ctaPad) + ctaMsg
421: 		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, banner, "", centeredCTA)
422: 	default:
423: 		shown := string(ctaRunes[:visChars])
424: 		remaining := ctaFullWidth - lipgloss.Width(shown)
425: 		partialCTA := strings.Repeat(" ", ctaPad) + shown + strings.Repeat(" ", remaining)
426: 		return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, banner, "", partialCTA)
427: 	}
428: }
429: 
430: // enterScrollMode captures the full terminal history and sets up the viewport
431: // for scroll mode. Shared by all scroll entry points.
432: func (p *PreviewPane) enterScrollMode(instance *session.Instance) error {
433: 	content, err := instance.PreviewFullHistory()
434: 	if err != nil {
435: 		return err
436: 	}
437: 	footer := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(ColorMuted).Render("ESC to exit scroll mode")
438: 	p.viewport.SetContent(lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, content, footer))
439: 	p.viewport.GotoBottom()
440: 	p.isScrolling = true
441: 	return nil
442: }
443: 
444: // ScrollUp scrolls the preview up one line. Enters scroll mode on first call.
445: func (p *PreviewPane) ScrollUp(instance *session.Instance) error {
446: 	if p.isDocument {
447: 		p.viewport.LineUp(1)
448: 		return nil
449: 	}
450: 	if instance == nil || instance.Status == session.Paused {
451: 		return nil
452: 	}
453: 	if !p.isScrolling {
454: 		if err := p.enterScrollMode(instance); err != nil {
455: 			return err
456: 		}
457: 		return nil
458: 	}
459: 	p.viewport.LineUp(1)
460: 	return nil
461: }
462: 
463: // ScrollDown scrolls the preview down one line. Enters scroll mode on first call.
464: func (p *PreviewPane) ScrollDown(instance *session.Instance) error {
465: 	if p.isDocument {
466: 		p.viewport.LineDown(1)
467: 		return nil
468: 	}
469: 	if instance == nil || instance.Status == session.Paused {
470: 		return nil
471: 	}
472: 	if !p.isScrolling {
473: 		if err := p.enterScrollMode(instance); err != nil {
474: 			return err
475: 		}
476: 		return nil
477: 	}
478: 	p.viewport.LineDown(1)
479: 	return nil
480: }
481: 
482: // HalfPageUp scrolls up half a viewport height. Enters scroll mode on first call.
483: func (p *PreviewPane) HalfPageUp(instance *session.Instance) error {
484: 	if p.isDocument {
485: 		p.viewport.HalfViewUp()
486: 		return nil
487: 	}
488: 	if instance == nil || instance.Status == session.Paused {
489: 		return nil
490: 	}
491: 	if !p.isScrolling {
492: 		if err := p.enterScrollMode(instance); err != nil {
493: 			return err
494: 		}
495: 	}
496: 	p.viewport.HalfViewUp()
497: 	return nil
498: }
499: 
500: // HalfPageDown scrolls down half a viewport height. Enters scroll mode on first call.
501: func (p *PreviewPane) HalfPageDown(instance *session.Instance) error {
502: 	if p.isDocument {
503: 		p.viewport.HalfViewDown()
504: 		return nil
505: 	}
506: 	if instance == nil || instance.Status == session.Paused {
507: 		return nil
508: 	}
509: 	if !p.isScrolling {
510: 		if err := p.enterScrollMode(instance); err != nil {
511: 			return err
512: 		}
513: 	}
514: 	p.viewport.HalfViewDown()
515: 	return nil
516: }
517: 
518: // ResetToNormalMode exits scroll mode and returns to live preview.
519: func (p *PreviewPane) ResetToNormalMode(instance *session.Instance) error {
520: 	if instance == nil || instance.Status == session.Paused {
521: 		return nil
522: 	}
523: 	if !p.isScrolling {
524: 		return nil
525: 	}
526: 	p.isScrolling = false
527: 	p.viewport.SetContent("")
528: 	p.viewport.GotoTop()
529: 
530: 	// Fetch fresh preview content immediately rather than waiting for next tick.
531: 	content, err := instance.Preview()
532: 	if err != nil {
533: 		return err
534: 	}
535: 	p.previewState.text = content
536: 	return nil
537: }

(End of file - total 537 lines)

139: // NewNavigationPanel creates a NavigationPanel with an attached spinner model.
140: func NewNavigationPanel(sp *spinner.Model) *NavigationPanel {
141: 	return &NavigationPanel{
142: 		spinner:        sp,
143: 		planStatuses:   make(map[string]TopicStatus),
144: 		collapsed:      make(map[string]bool),
145: 		userOverrides:  make(map[string]bool),

152: // ---------- data setters ----------
153: 
154: // SetData is the primary data update path. It replaces all state and rebuilds rows.
155: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetData(
156: 	plans []PlanDisplay,
157: 	instances []*session.Instance,
158: 	history []PlanDisplay,
159: 	cancelled []PlanDisplay,
160: 	planStatuses map[string]TopicStatus,
161: ) {
162: 	n.plans = plans
163: 	n.instances = instances

167: 	} else {

169: 	}
170: 	n.splitDeadFromHistory(history)
171: 	n.rebuildRows()
172: }

180: // SetTopicsAndPlans replaces topics, ungrouped plans, history, and optionally
181: // cancelled plans, then rebuilds rows.
182: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetTopicsAndPlans(
183: 	topics []TopicDisplay,
184: 	ungrouped []PlanDisplay,
185: 	history []PlanDisplay,
186: 	cancelled ...[]PlanDisplay,
187: ) {

191: 	}
192: 	all := make([]PlanDisplay, 0, len(ungrouped))
193: 	all = append(all, ungrouped...)
194: 	for _, t := range topics {
195: 		all = append(all, t.Plans...)
196: 	}
197: 	n.plans = all
198: 	n.splitDeadFromHistory(history)
199: 	n.rebuildRows()

206: 	}
207: }

211: 	if planStatuses != nil {

217: // ---------- dead/history partitioning ----------
218: 
219: // splitDeadFromHistory partitions a finished plan list into three buckets:
220: //   - promoted (added to n.plans): plan has at least one running/loading instance
221: //   - dead:     plan has only non-running instances, or was manually inspected
222: //   - history:  plan has no instances at all
223: //
224: // Previously promoted plans are removed from n.plans before re-partitioning.
225: func (n *NavigationPanel) splitDeadFromHistory(finished []PlanDisplay) {
226: 	// Build per-plan instance info from current instance list.
227: 	type info struct{ hasAny, hasRunning bool }
228: 	byPlan := make(map[string]info, len(n.instances))
229: 	for _, inst := range n.instances {
230: 		if inst.TaskFile == "" {
231: 			continue
232: 		}
233: 		entry := byPlan[inst.TaskFile]
234: 		entry.hasAny = true
235: 		if inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading {
236: 			entry.hasRunning = true
237: 		}
238: 		byPlan[inst.TaskFile] = entry
239: 	}
240: 
241: 	// Strip previously promoted plans from n.plans to avoid duplicates.
242: 	if len(n.promotedPlans) > 0 {
243: 		promoted := make(map[string]bool, len(n.promotedPlans))
244: 		for _, p := range n.promotedPlans {
245: 			promoted[p.Filename] = true
246: 		}
247: 		kept := n.plans[:0]
248: 		for _, p := range n.plans {
249: 			if !promoted[p.Filename] {
250: 				kept = append(kept, p)
251: 			}
252: 		}
253: 		n.plans = kept
254: 	}
255: 
256: 	n.deadPlans = nil
257: 	n.historyPlans = nil
258: 	n.promotedPlans = nil
259: 
260: 	for _, p := range finished {
261: 		inf := byPlan[p.Filename]
262: 		switch {
263: 		case inf.hasRunning:
264: 			// Running instances → promote into active plans.
265: 			n.promotedPlans = append(n.promotedPlans, p)
266: 			n.plans = append(n.plans, p)
267: 		case inf.hasAny || n.inspectedPlans[p.Filename]:
268: 			// Non-running instances or manually inspected → dead section.
269: 			n.deadPlans = append(n.deadPlans, p)
270: 		default:
271: 			// No instances → history.
272: 			n.historyPlans = append(n.historyPlans, p)
273: 		}
274: 	}
275: }
276: 
277: // resplitDead recombines all finished plan buckets and re-partitions them.
278: func (n *NavigationPanel) resplitDead() {
279: 	all := make([]PlanDisplay, 0, len(n.deadPlans)+len(n.promotedPlans)+len(n.historyPlans))
280: 	all = append(all, n.deadPlans...)
281: 	all = append(all, n.promotedPlans...)
282: 	all = append(all, n.historyPlans...)
283: 	n.splitDeadFromHistory(all)
284: }
285: 
286: // InspectPlan marks a history plan for manual inspection and moves it to dead.
287: func (n *NavigationPanel) InspectPlan(planFile string) {
288: 	if n.inspectedPlans == nil {
289: 		n.inspectedPlans = make(map[string]bool)
290: 	}
291: 	n.inspectedPlans[planFile] = true
292: 	n.resplitDead()
293: 	n.deadExpanded = true
294: 	n.rebuildRows()
295: }
296: 
297: // ---------- row building ----------
298: 
299: // rebuildRows reconstructs the rows slice from current state. Selection is
300: // preserved by ID where possible, or clamped to the previous numeric position.
301: func (n *NavigationPanel) rebuildRows() {
302: 	// Capture previous selection ID for restore.
303: 	prevID := ""
304: 	prevIdx := n.selectedIdx
305: 	if prevIdx >= 0 && prevIdx < len(n.rows) {
306: 		prevID = n.rows[prevIdx].ID
307: 	}
308: 
309: 	// Partition instances into plan-attached and solo.
310: 	byPlan := make(map[string][]*session.Instance)
311: 	var solo []*session.Instance
312: 	for _, inst := range n.instances {
313: 		if inst.TaskFile == "" {
314: 			solo = append(solo, inst)
315: 		} else {
316: 			byPlan[inst.TaskFile] = append(byPlan[inst.TaskFile], inst)
317: 		}
318: 	}
319: 
320: 	// Sort helpers
321: 	sortInsts := func(list []*session.Instance) {
322: 		sort.SliceStable(list, func(i, j int) bool {
323: 			ki, kj := navInstanceSortKey(list[i]), navInstanceSortKey(list[j])
324: 			if ki != kj {
325: 				return ki < kj
326: 			}
327: 			return strings.ToLower(list[i].Title) < strings.ToLower(list[j].Title)
328: 		})
329: 	}
330: 	for _, list := range byPlan {
331: 		sortInsts(list)
332: 	}
333: 	sortInsts(solo)
334: 
335: 	// Sort plans: notification (0) < running/active-status (1) < idle (2), then alpha.
336: 	sorted := append([]PlanDisplay(nil), n.plans...)
337: 	sort.SliceStable(sorted, func(i, j int) bool {
338: 		pi, pj := sorted[i], sorted[j]
339: 		ki := navPlanSortKey(pi, byPlan[pi.Filename], n.planStatuses[pi.Filename])
340: 		kj := navPlanSortKey(pj, byPlan[pj.Filename], n.planStatuses[pj.Filename])
341: 		if ki != kj {
342: 			return ki < kj
343: 		}
344: 		return strings.ToLower(taskstate.DisplayName(pi.Filename)) < strings.ToLower(taskstate.DisplayName(pj.Filename))
345: 	})
346: 
347: 	capacity := len(sorted) + len(n.instances) + len(n.deadPlans) + len(n.historyPlans) + len(n.cancelled) + 8
348: 	rows := make([]navRow, 0, capacity)
349: 
350: 	// appendPlan emits a plan header row and optionally its child instance rows.
351: 	appendPlan := func(p PlanDisplay, indent int) {
352: 		insts := byPlan[p.Filename]
353: 		hasRunning, hasNotif := aggregateNavPlanStatus(insts, n.planStatuses[p.Filename])
354: 		collapsed := n.isPlanCollapsed(p.Filename, hasRunning, hasNotif)
355: 		rows = append(rows, navRow{
356: 			Kind:            navRowPlanHeader,
357: 			ID:              SidebarPlanPrefix + p.Filename,
358: 			Label:           taskstate.DisplayName(p.Filename),
359: 			TaskFile:        p.Filename,
360: 			PlanStatus:      p.Status,
361: 			Collapsed:       collapsed,
362: 			HasRunning:      hasRunning,
363: 			HasNotification: hasNotif,
364: 			Indent:          indent,
365: 		})
366: 		if !collapsed {
367: 			for _, inst := range insts {
368: 				rows = append(rows, navRow{
369: 					Kind:     navRowInstance,
370: 					ID:       "inst:" + inst.Title,
371: 					Label:    inst.Title,
372: 					TaskFile: inst.TaskFile,
373: 					Instance: inst,
374: 					Indent:   indent,
375: 				})
376: 			}
377: 		}
378: 	}
379: 
380: 	// ClickUp import action (pinned at top when available).
381: 	if n.clickUpAvail {
382: 		rows = append(rows, navRow{
383: 			Kind:  navRowImportAction,
384: 			ID:    SidebarImportClickUp,
385: 			Label: "+ import from clickup",
386: 		})
387: 	}
388: 
389: 	// Dead section: plans with non-running instances or manually inspected.
390: 	if len(n.deadPlans) > 0 {
391: 		rows = append(rows, navRow{
392: 			Kind:      navRowDeadToggle,
393: 			ID:        "__dead_toggle__",
394: 			Label:     "dead",
395: 			Collapsed: !n.deadExpanded,
396: 		})
397: 		if n.deadExpanded {
398: 			for _, p := range n.deadPlans {
399: 				appendPlan(p, 2)
400: 			}
401: 		}
402: 	}
403: 
404: 	// Split sorted plans into active (key < 2) and idle (key == 2).
405: 	var activePlans, idlePlans []PlanDisplay
406: 	for _, p := range sorted {
407: 		if navPlanSortKey(p, byPlan[p.Filename], n.planStatuses[p.Filename]) < 2 {
408: 			activePlans = append(activePlans, p)
409: 		} else {
410: 			idlePlans = append(idlePlans, p)
411: 		}
412: 	}
413: 
414: 	// Active plans (running, notified, implementing, reviewing).
415: 	for _, p := range activePlans {
416: 		appendPlan(p, 0)
417: 	}
418: 
419: 	// Solo instances section (between active plans and idle plans).
420: 	if len(solo) > 0 {
421: 		rows = append(rows, navRow{Kind: navRowSoloHeader, ID: "__solo__", Label: "agents"})
422: 		for _, inst := range solo {
423: 			rows = append(rows, navRow{
424: 				Kind:     navRowInstance,
425: 				ID:       "inst:" + inst.Title,
426: 				Label:    inst.Title,
427: 				Instance: inst,
428: 			})
429: 		}
430: 	}
431: 
432: 	// Idle plans grouped by topic, then ungrouped remainder.
433: 	idleSet := make(map[string]bool, len(idlePlans))
434: 	for _, p := range idlePlans {
435: 		idleSet[p.Filename] = true
436: 	}
437: 
438: 	emitted := make(map[string]bool)
439: 	for _, t := range n.topics {
440: 		var planGroup []PlanDisplay
441: 		for _, p := range t.Plans {
442: 			if idleSet[p.Filename] {
443: 				planGroup = append(planGroup, p)
444: 			}
445: 		}
446: 		if len(planGroup) == 0 {
447: 			continue
448: 		}
449: 		topicID := SidebarTopicPrefix + t.Name
450: 		collapsed := n.collapsed[topicID]
451: 		rows = append(rows, navRow{
452: 			Kind:      navRowTopicHeader,
453: 			ID:        topicID,
454: 			Label:     t.Name,
455: 			Collapsed: collapsed,
456: 		})
457: 		for _, p := range planGroup {
458: 			emitted[p.Filename] = true
459: 			if !collapsed {
460: 				appendPlan(p, 2)
461: 			}
462: 		}
463: 	}
464: 
465: 	// Ungrouped idle plans.
466: 	for _, p := range idlePlans {
467: 		if !emitted[p.Filename] {
468: 			appendPlan(p, 0)
469: 		}
470: 	}
471: 
472: 	// History section (collapsed toggle, expands to list).
473: 	if len(n.historyPlans) > 0 {
474: 		rows = append(rows, navRow{
475: 			Kind:      navRowHistoryToggle,
476: 			ID:        SidebarPlanHistoryToggle,
477: 			Label:     "history",
478: 			Collapsed: !n.historyExpanded,
479: 		})
480: 		if n.historyExpanded {
481: 			for _, p := range n.historyPlans {
482: 				rows = append(rows, navRow{
483: 					Kind:     navRowHistoryPlan,
484: 					ID:       SidebarPlanPrefix + p.Filename,
485: 					Label:    taskstate.DisplayName(p.Filename),
486: 					TaskFile: p.Filename,
487: 				})
488: 			}
489: 		}
490: 	}
491: 
492: 	// Cancelled plans (always shown, no toggle).
493: 	for _, p := range n.cancelled {
494: 		rows = append(rows, navRow{
495: 			Kind:     navRowCancelled,
496: 			ID:       SidebarPlanPrefix + p.Filename,
497: 			Label:    taskstate.DisplayName(p.Filename),
498: 			TaskFile: p.Filename,
499: 		})
500: 	}
501: 
502: 	n.rows = rows
503: 
504: 	if len(rows) == 0 {
505: 		n.selectedIdx = 0
506: 		n.scrollOffset = 0
507: 		return
508: 	}
509: 
510: 	// Restore selection by ID.
511: 	if prevID != "" {
512: 		for i, row := range rows {
513: 			if row.ID == prevID {
514: 				n.selectedIdx = i
515: 				n.clampScroll()
516: 				return
517: 			}
518: 		}
519: 	}
520: 
521: 	// ID not found — clamp to previous numeric position, skipping dividers.
522: 	if prevIdx >= len(rows) {
523: 		prevIdx = len(rows) - 1
524: 	}
525: 	if prevIdx < 0 {
526: 		prevIdx = 0
527: 	}
528: 	n.selectedIdx = prevIdx
529: 	if n.selectedIdx < len(rows) && rows[n.selectedIdx].Kind == navRowSoloHeader {
530: 		if n.selectedIdx+1 < len(rows) {
531: 			n.selectedIdx++
532: 		} else if n.selectedIdx > 0 {
533: 			n.selectedIdx--
534: 		}
535: 	}
536: 	n.clampScroll()
537: }
538: 
539: // ---------- sort key helpers ----------
540: 
541: // navInstanceSortKey returns the sort priority for an instance within a plan.
542: // Lower values sort first: running (0) < notified (1) < paused (2) < done (3).
543: func navInstanceSortKey(inst *session.Instance) int {
544: 	if inst.ImplementationComplete {
545: 		return 3
546: 	}
547: 	switch inst.Status {
548: 	case session.Running, session.Loading:
549: 		return 0
550: 	case session.Paused:
551: 		return 2
552: 	}
553: 	if inst.Notified {
554: 		return 1
555: 	}
556: 	return 3
557: }
558: 
559: // navPlanSortKey returns the sort priority for a plan.
560: // 0 = has notification, 1 = running or active lifecycle, 2 = idle.
561: func navPlanSortKey(p PlanDisplay, insts []*session.Instance, st TopicStatus) int {
562: 	hasNotif := st.HasNotification
563: 	hasRunning := st.HasRunning
564: 	for _, inst := range insts {
565: 		if inst.Notified {
566: 			hasNotif = true
567: 		}
568: 		if inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading {
569: 			hasRunning = true
570: 		}
571: 	}
572: 	switch {
573: 	case hasNotif:
574: 		return 0
575: 	case hasRunning, p.Status == "implementing", p.Status == "reviewing":
576: 		return 1
577: 	default:
578: 		return 2
579: 	}
580: }
581: 
582: // aggregateNavPlanStatus derives combined running/notification flags from
583: // instance state and stored plan status flags.
584: func aggregateNavPlanStatus(insts []*session.Instance, st TopicStatus) (hasRunning, hasNotif bool) {
585: 	hasRunning = st.HasRunning
586: 	hasNotif = st.HasNotification
587: 	for _, inst := range insts {
588: 		if inst.Notified {
589: 			hasNotif = true
590: 		}
591: 		if inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading {
592: 			hasRunning = true
593: 		}
594: 	}
595: 	return
596: }
597: 
598: // isPlanCollapsed returns whether a plan should be shown collapsed.
599: // User overrides always win; otherwise collapse unless running or notified.
600: func (n *NavigationPanel) isPlanCollapsed(planFile string, hasRunning, hasNotif bool) bool {
601: 	if _, ok := n.userOverrides[planFile]; ok {
602: 		return n.collapsed[planFile]
603: 	}
604: 	return !hasRunning && !hasNotif
605: }

607: // ---------- layout ----------
608: 
609: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetSize(width, height int) {
610: 	n.width, n.height = width, height
611: 	n.clampScroll()
612: }
613: 
614: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetAuditView(view string, h int) {
615: 	n.auditView = view
616: 	n.auditHeight = h
617: }
618: 
619: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetFocused(focused bool)    { n.focused = focused }
620: func (n *NavigationPanel) IsFocused() bool            { return n.focused }
621: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetClickUpAvailable(a bool) { n.clickUpAvail = a; n.rebuildRows() }
622: 
623: // availRows returns the number of rows the scroll window can display.
624: func (n *NavigationPanel) availRows() int {
625: 	v := n.height - 8
626: 	if v < 1 {
627: 		return 1
628: 	}
629: 	return v
630: }
631: 
632: // clampScroll ensures scrollOffset keeps selectedIdx visible.
633: func (n *NavigationPanel) clampScroll() {
634: 	if len(n.rows) == 0 {
635: 		n.scrollOffset = 0
636: 		return
637: 	}
638: 	avail := n.availRows()
639: 	if n.selectedIdx < n.scrollOffset {
640: 		n.scrollOffset = n.selectedIdx
641: 	}
642: 	if n.selectedIdx >= n.scrollOffset+avail {
643: 		n.scrollOffset = n.selectedIdx - avail + 1
644: 	}
645: 	if n.scrollOffset < 0 {
646: 		n.scrollOffset = 0
647: 	}
648: }

650: // ---------- search ----------
651: 
652: func (n *NavigationPanel) ActivateSearch()        { n.searchActive = true; n.searchQuery = "" }
653: func (n *NavigationPanel) DeactivateSearch()      { n.searchActive = false; n.searchQuery = "" }
654: func (n *NavigationPanel) IsSearchActive() bool   { return n.searchActive }
655: func (n *NavigationPanel) GetSearchQuery() string { return n.searchQuery }
656: 
657: // SetSearchQuery updates the search filter and snaps selection to the first matching row.
658: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetSearchQuery(q string) {
659: 	n.searchQuery = q
660: 	if q != "" && len(n.rows) > 0 && !n.rowMatchesSearch(n.selectedIdx) {
661: 		for i := range n.rows {
662: 			if n.rows[i].Kind != navRowSoloHeader && n.rowMatchesSearch(i) {
663: 				n.selectedIdx = i
664: 				n.clampScroll()
665: 				return
666: 			}
667: 		}
668: 	}
669: }
670: 
671: // rowMatchesSearch returns true if the row at idx passes the current search filter.
672: func (n *NavigationPanel) rowMatchesSearch(idx int) bool {
673: 	if !n.searchActive || n.searchQuery == "" {
674: 		return true
675: 	}
676: 	q := strings.ToLower(n.searchQuery)
677: 	row := n.rows[idx]
678: 	return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(row.Label), q) ||
679: 		strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(row.TaskFile), q)
680: }
681: 
682: // ---------- expand/collapse ----------
683: 
684: // ToggleSelectedExpand toggles the expand/collapse state of the selected row.
685: // Returns true if the row supports toggling.
686: func (n *NavigationPanel) ToggleSelectedExpand() bool {
687: 	if n.selectedIdx < 0 || n.selectedIdx >= len(n.rows) {
688: 		return false
689: 	}
690: 	row := n.rows[n.selectedIdx]
691: 	switch row.Kind {
692: 	case navRowPlanHeader:
693: 		n.collapsed[row.TaskFile] = !row.Collapsed
694: 		n.userOverrides[row.TaskFile] = true
695: 		n.rebuildRows()
696: 		return true
697: 	case navRowTopicHeader:
698: 		n.collapsed[row.ID] = !row.Collapsed
699: 		n.rebuildRows()
700: 		return true
701: 	case navRowDeadToggle:
702: 		n.deadExpanded = !n.deadExpanded
703: 		n.rebuildRows()
704: 		return true
705: 	case navRowHistoryToggle:
706: 		n.historyExpanded = !n.historyExpanded
707: 		n.rebuildRows()
708: 		return true
709: 	default:
710: 		return false
711: 	}
712: }
713: 
714: // ---------- navigation ----------
715: 
716: // Up moves the selection up one visible, selectable row.
717: func (n *NavigationPanel) Up() {
718: 	orig := n.selectedIdx
719: 	for n.selectedIdx > 0 {
720: 		n.selectedIdx--
721: 		if n.rows[n.selectedIdx].Kind == navRowSoloHeader {
722: 			continue
723: 		}
724: 		if !n.rowMatchesSearch(n.selectedIdx) {
725: 			continue
726: 		}
727: 		n.clampScroll()
728: 		return
729: 	}
730: 	n.selectedIdx = orig
731: }
732: 
733: // Down moves the selection down one visible, selectable row.
734: func (n *NavigationPanel) Down() {
735: 	orig := n.selectedIdx
736: 	for n.selectedIdx+1 < len(n.rows) {
737: 		n.selectedIdx++
738: 		if n.rows[n.selectedIdx].Kind == navRowSoloHeader {
739: 			continue
740: 		}
741: 		if !n.rowMatchesSearch(n.selectedIdx) {
742: 			continue
743: 		}
744: 		n.clampScroll()
745: 		return
746: 	}
747: 	n.selectedIdx = orig
748: }
749: 
750: // Left collapses an expanded plan header, or jumps to the parent header when on an instance.
751: func (n *NavigationPanel) Left() {
752: 	if n.selectedIdx < 0 || n.selectedIdx >= len(n.rows) {
753: 		return
754: 	}
755: 	row := n.rows[n.selectedIdx]
756: 	switch row.Kind {
757: 	case navRowPlanHeader:
758: 		if !row.Collapsed {
759: 			n.ToggleSelectedExpand()
760: 		}
761: 	case navRowInstance:
762: 		for i := n.selectedIdx - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
763: 			if n.rows[i].Kind == navRowPlanHeader {
764: 				n.selectedIdx = i
765: 				n.clampScroll()
766: 				return
767: 			}
768: 		}
769: 	}
770: }
771: 
772: // Right expands a collapsed header, or descends into the first child row.
773: func (n *NavigationPanel) Right() {
774: 	if n.selectedIdx < 0 || n.selectedIdx >= len(n.rows) {
775: 		return
776: 	}
777: 	row := n.rows[n.selectedIdx]
778: 	switch row.Kind {
779: 	case navRowPlanHeader:
780: 		if row.Collapsed {
781: 			n.ToggleSelectedExpand()
782: 		} else {
783: 			n.Down()
784: 		}
785: 	case navRowDeadToggle:
786: 		if row.Collapsed {
787: 			n.ToggleSelectedExpand()
788: 		} else {
789: 			n.Down()
790: 		}
791: 	case navRowHistoryToggle:
792: 		if row.Collapsed {
793: 			n.ToggleSelectedExpand()
794: 		}
795: 	}
796: }

798: // ---------- selection API ----------
799: 
800: func (n *NavigationPanel) GetSelectedInstance() *session.Instance {
801: 	if n.selectedIdx < 0 || n.selectedIdx >= len(n.rows) {
802: 		return nil
803: 	}

816: 		return false
817: 	}
818: 	k := n.rows[n.selectedIdx].Kind
819: 	return k == navRowPlanHeader || k == navRowHistoryPlan || k == navRowCancelled
820: }
821: 
822: func (n *NavigationPanel) IsSelectedHistoryPlan() bool {
823: 	if n.selectedIdx < 0 || n.selectedIdx >= len(n.rows) {
824: 		return false
825: 	}
826: 	return n.rows[n.selectedIdx].Kind == navRowHistoryPlan

829: func (n *NavigationPanel) GetSelectedID() string {
830: 	if n.selectedIdx < 0 || n.selectedIdx >= len(n.rows) {

833: 	return n.rows[n.selectedIdx].ID

836: func (n *NavigationPanel) GetSelectedIdx() int  { return n.selectedIdx }
837: func (n *NavigationPanel) GetScrollOffset() int { return n.scrollOffset }
838: 
839: // ClickItem selects a row by its raw index.
840: func (n *NavigationPanel) ClickItem(row int) {
841: 	if row >= 0 && row < len(n.rows) {
842: 		n.selectedIdx = row
843: 		n.clampScroll()
844: 	}
845: }
846: 
847: // SelectByID moves selection to the row with the given ID. Returns false if not found.
848: func (n *NavigationPanel) SelectByID(id string) bool {
849: 	for i, row := range n.rows {
850: 		if row.ID == id {
851: 			n.selectedIdx = i
852: 			n.clampScroll()
853: 			return true
854: 		}
855: 	}
856: 	return false
857: }
858: 
859: // SelectInstance moves selection to the given instance, auto-expanding its plan if needed.
860: func (n *NavigationPanel) SelectInstance(inst *session.Instance) bool {
861: 	for i, row := range n.rows {
862: 		if row.Instance == inst {
863: 			n.selectedIdx = i
864: 			n.clampScroll()
865: 			return true
866: 		}
867: 	}
868: 	// Instance is under a collapsed plan — expand and retry.
869: 	if inst.TaskFile != "" {
870: 		n.collapsed[inst.TaskFile] = false
871: 		n.userOverrides[inst.TaskFile] = true
872: 		n.rebuildRows()
873: 		for i, row := range n.rows {
874: 			if row.Instance == inst {
875: 				n.selectedIdx = i
876: 				n.clampScroll()
877: 				return true
878: 			}
879: 		}
880: 	}
881: 	return false
882: }
883: 
884: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetSelectedInstance(idx int) {
885: 	if idx < 0 || idx >= len(n.instances) {
886: 		return
887: 	}
888: 	_ = n.SelectInstance(n.instances[idx])
889: }
890: 
891: func (n *NavigationPanel) SelectedIndex() int {
892: 	inst := n.GetSelectedInstance()
893: 	if inst == nil {
894: 		return 0
895: 	}
896: 	for i, item := range n.instances {
897: 		if item == inst {
898: 			return i
899: 		}
900: 	}
901: 	return 0
902: }
903: 
904: // SelectFirst selects the first selectable row (skips solo-header dividers).
905: func (n *NavigationPanel) SelectFirst() {
906: 	if len(n.rows) == 0 {
907: 		return
908: 	}
909: 	n.selectedIdx = 0
910: 	if n.rows[0].Kind == navRowSoloHeader && len(n.rows) > 1 {
911: 		n.selectedIdx = 1
912: 	}
913: 	n.clampScroll()
914: }
915: 
916: // SelectedSpaceAction returns the label for the space-key action on the current row.
917: func (n *NavigationPanel) SelectedSpaceAction() string {
918: 	if n.selectedIdx < 0 || n.selectedIdx >= len(n.rows) {
919: 		return "toggle"
920: 	}
921: 	switch n.rows[n.selectedIdx].Kind {
922: 	case navRowPlanHeader, navRowDeadToggle, navRowHistoryToggle:
923: 		if n.rows[n.selectedIdx].Collapsed {
924: 			return "expand"
925: 		}
926: 		return "collapse"
927: 	default:
928: 		return "toggle"
929: 	}
930: }
931: 
932: // ---------- cycle ----------
933: 
934: // CycleNextActive moves selection to the next non-paused instance in visual order.
935: func (n *NavigationPanel) CycleNextActive() { n.cycleActive(1) }
936: 
937: // CyclePrevActive moves selection to the previous non-paused instance in visual order.
938: func (n *NavigationPanel) CyclePrevActive() { n.cycleActive(-1) }
939: 
940: // cycleActive builds an ordered list of non-paused instances (including those
941: // hidden under collapsed plans at their header position), then advances by step.
942: func (n *NavigationPanel) cycleActive(step int) {
943: 	var ordered []*session.Instance
944: 	for _, row := range n.rows {
945: 		switch {
946: 		case row.Kind == navRowInstance && row.Instance != nil && !row.Instance.Paused():
947: 			ordered = append(ordered, row.Instance)
948: 		case row.Kind == navRowPlanHeader && row.Collapsed:
949: 			for _, inst := range n.instances {
950: 				if inst.TaskFile == row.TaskFile && !inst.Paused() {
951: 					ordered = append(ordered, inst)
952: 				}
953: 			}
954: 		}
955: 	}
956: 	if len(ordered) == 0 {
957: 		return
958: 	}
959: 
960: 	cur := -1
961: 	if sel := n.GetSelectedInstance(); sel != nil {
962: 		for i, inst := range ordered {
963: 			if inst == sel {
964: 				cur = i
965: 				break
966: 			}
967: 		}
968: 	}
969: 	if cur < 0 {
970: 		cur = 0
971: 	}
972: 	next := (cur + step + len(ordered)) % len(ordered)
973: 	n.SelectInstance(ordered[next])
974: }
975: 
976: // ---------- instance management ----------
977: 
978: func (n *NavigationPanel) GetInstances() []*session.Instance { return n.instances }
979: func (n *NavigationPanel) TotalInstances() int               { return len(n.instances) }
980: func (n *NavigationPanel) NumInstances() int                 { return len(n.instances) }
981: 
982: // AddInstance appends an instance and returns a no-op cleanup function.
983: func (n *NavigationPanel) AddInstance(inst *session.Instance) func() {
984: 	n.instances = append(n.instances, inst)
985: 	n.resplitDead()
986: 	n.rebuildRows()
987: 	return func() {}
988: }
989: 
990: // RemoveByTitle removes the instance with the given title and returns it (nil if not found).
991: func (n *NavigationPanel) RemoveByTitle(title string) *session.Instance {
992: 	for i, inst := range n.instances {
993: 		if inst.Title == title {
994: 			n.instances = append(n.instances[:i], n.instances[i+1:]...)
995: 			n.resplitDead()
996: 			n.rebuildRows()
997: 			return inst
998: 		}
999: 	}
1000: 	return nil
1001: }
1002: 
1003: // Remove removes the currently selected instance.
1004: func (n *NavigationPanel) Remove() {
1005: 	if inst := n.GetSelectedInstance(); inst != nil {
1006: 		n.RemoveByTitle(inst.Title)
1007: 	}
1008: }
1009: 
1010: // Kill sends SIGKILL to the selected instance and removes it.
1011: func (n *NavigationPanel) Kill() {
1012: 	if inst := n.GetSelectedInstance(); inst != nil {
1013: 		_ = inst.Kill()
1014: 		n.RemoveByTitle(inst.Title)
1015: 	}
1016: }
1017: 
1018: // Attach opens an interactive terminal session for the selected instance.
1019: func (n *NavigationPanel) Attach() (chan struct{}, error) {
1020: 	inst := n.GetSelectedInstance()
1021: 	if inst == nil {
1022: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no instance selected")
1023: 	}
1024: 	return inst.Attach()
1025: }
1026: 
1027: // Clear removes all instances and resets the row list.
1028: func (n *NavigationPanel) Clear() {
1029: 	n.instances = nil
1030: 	n.rows = nil
1031: 	n.selectedIdx = 0
1032: 	n.scrollOffset = 0
1033: }
1034: 
1035: // SetSessionPreviewSize resizes all active (non-paused) instance preview panes.
1036: func (n *NavigationPanel) SetSessionPreviewSize(width, height int) error {
1037: 	var firstErr error
1038: 	for _, inst := range n.instances {
1039: 		if !inst.Started() || inst.Paused() {
1040: 			continue
1041: 		}
1042: 		if err := inst.SetPreviewSize(width, height); err != nil && firstErr == nil {
1043: 			firstErr = err
1044: 		}
1045: 	}
1046: 	return firstErr
1047: }
1048: 
1049: // FindPlanInstance returns the best candidate instance for a plan: prefers
1050: // running/loading over ready. Returns nil if only paused instances exist.
1051: func (n *NavigationPanel) FindPlanInstance(planFile string) *session.Instance {
1052: 	var candidate *session.Instance
1053: 	for _, inst := range n.instances {
1054: 		if inst.TaskFile != planFile || inst.Paused() {
1055: 			continue
1056: 		}
1057: 		if inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading {
1058: 			return inst // running wins immediately
1059: 		}
1060: 		if candidate == nil {
1061: 			candidate = inst
1062: 		}
1063: 	}
1064: 	return candidate
1065: }
1066: 
1067: // RowCount returns the total number of rows in the panel.
1068: func (n *NavigationPanel) RowCount() int { return len(n.rows) }
1069: 
1070: // ---------- display helpers ----------
1071: 
1072: // navInstanceTitle returns the human-readable display label for an instance.
1073: func navInstanceTitle(inst *session.Instance) string {
1074: 	switch {
1075: 	case inst.WaveNumber > 0 && inst.TaskNumber > 0:
1076: 		return fmt.Sprintf("wave %d · task %d", inst.WaveNumber, inst.TaskNumber)
1077: 	case inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer && inst.TaskFile != "":
1078: 		return "review"
1079: 	case inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypePlanner && inst.TaskFile != "":
1080: 		return "planning"
1081: 	case inst.SoloAgent && inst.TaskFile != "":
1082: 		return taskstate.DisplayName(inst.TaskFile)
1083: 	case inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypeCoder && inst.TaskFile != "" && inst.WaveNumber == 0:
1084: 		return "applying fixes"
1085: 	default:
1086: 		return inst.Title
1087: 	}
1088: }
1089: 
1090: // navInstanceStatusIcon returns a styled status glyph for an instance row.
1091: func (n *NavigationPanel) navInstanceStatusIcon(inst *session.Instance) string {
1092: 	if inst.Exited {
1093: 		return navCancelledLblStyle.Render("✕")
1094: 	}
1095: 	if inst.ImplementationComplete {
1096: 		return navCompletedIconStyle.Render("✓")
1097: 	}
1098: 	switch inst.Status {
1099: 	case session.Running, session.Loading:
1100: 		if n.spinner != nil {
1101: 			return strings.TrimRight(n.spinner.View(), " ")
1102: 		}
1103: 		return navRunningIconStyle.Render("●")
1104: 	case session.Ready:
1105: 		if inst.Notified {
1106: 			return navNotifyIconStyle.Render("◉")
1107: 		}
1108: 		return navReadyIconStyle.Render("●")
1109: 	case session.Paused:
1110: 		return navPausedIconStyle.Render("\uf04c")
1111: 	default:
1112: 		return navIdleIconStyle.Render("○")
1113: 	}
1114: }

1116: // navPlanStatusIcon returns a styled status glyph for a plan header row.
1117: func navPlanStatusIcon(row navRow) string {
1118: 	switch {
1119: 	case row.HasNotification:
1120: 		return navNotifyIconStyle.Render("◉")
1121: 	case row.HasRunning:
1122: 		return navRunningIconStyle.Render("●")
1123: 	case row.PlanStatus == "planning":
1124: 		return navRunningIconStyle.Render("●")
1125: 	case row.PlanStatus == "reviewing":
1126: 		return navNotifyIconStyle.Render("◉")
1127: 	default:
1128: 		return navIdleIconStyle.Render("○")
1129: 	}
1130: }

1133: func navSectionLabel(key int) string {
1134: 	if key < 2 {
1135: 		return "active"
1136: 	}
1137: 	return "plans"
1138: }

1140: // navDividerLine renders a full-width divider: "──── label ────".
1141: func navDividerLine(label string, w int) string {
1142: 	inner := " " + label + " "
1143: 	innerW := runewidth.StringWidth(inner)
1144: 	remaining := w - innerW
1145: 	if remaining < 2 {
1146: 		return navHistoryDivStyle.Render(inner)
1147: 	}
1148: 	left := remaining / 2
1149: 	right := remaining - left
1150: 	line := strings.Repeat("─", left) + inner + strings.Repeat("─", right)
1151: 	return navHistoryDivStyle.Render(line)
1152: }
